messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online course: Data Exploration, Regression, GLM & GAM. With intro to R
messages.subject_clean: Online course: Data Exploration, Regression, GLM & GAM. With intro to R
messages.body_plain:
We still have some places available on the following online statistics 
course:

*Course*: Data Exploration, Regression, GLM & GAM. With intro to R
*Dates*: 16-20 June 2025.
*Teaching times*: 08.30-15.30 BST
*Flyer*: www.highstat.com/Courses/Flyers/Flyer2025_06_RGG .pdf
*Registration*: www.highstat.com

*Course content*: We will begin with a brief intro to R and provide a 
step-by-step guide for data exploration, helping you avoid common 
statistical mistakes. You?ll learn how to identify outliers, manage 
collinearity, visualise relationships, and understand dependency 
structures. We will also explain why checking for normality at this 
stage is unnecessary.

We will then move on to multiple linear regression, a key statistical 
tool. We?ll cover the basics of linear regression using examples from 
biology, discuss potential challenges, and introduce the concept of 
interactions. We will demonstrate how generalised linear models (GLMs) 
can be used to analyse count data, presence-absence data, proportional 
data, and continuous data. Some key distributions will be explored in 
detail, along with a clear overview of all GLMs.

We will also discuss and apply generalised additive models (GAMs) to 
handle non-linear relationships. We will cover smoother-factor 
interactions and explore model selection within GAMs.

Throughout the course, there will be around 15 exercises to help you 
apply what you?ve learned.


Kind regards,

Alain Zuur



	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Using AI for Data Analysis in R  - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using AI for Data Analysis in R  - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Using AI for Data Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r->, running July 1st and by professor Daisung Jang from the University of Melbourne. Integrating generative AI tools such as ChatGPT and OpenRouter into your R workflow can save time, reduce errors, and produce compelling visualizations, and this hands-on workshop shows you exactly how. Through interactive sessions you will learn to generate R code for cleaning, manipulating, and analysing data, create publication-ready tables and graphics, automate repetitive tasks with custom functions, compare and select packages, and annotate your scripts for transparent reporting. Whether you are a PhD student, an academic, or a professional researcher, mastering these techniques will streamline your research pipeline, boost productivity, and elevate the quality of your empirical work. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r-> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Multivariate Tools for Ecologists – Get to Grips with the Vegan Package
messages.subject_clean: Multivariate Tools for Ecologists – Get to Grips with the Vegan Package
messages.body_plain:
*Multivariate Tools for Ecologists ? Get to Grips with the Vegan Package*

Want to analyse species composition, community structure, and ecological
gradients?

Join our in-depth course: *Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities
Using R With The VEGAN package (VGNR08)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr08/>*

*Course dates:* 15-19th September 2025

*Location:* Live and Online
Instructor: Dr. Antoine Becker-Scarpitta
This course is designed for ecologists and environmental scientists who
want to apply multivariate techniques using R?s*vegan* package. You?ll gain
hands-on experience with the tools used to interpret species-environment
relationships and community composition.

Taught by expert instructors, you?ll learn to:

   -

   Understand the principles of multivariate analysis
   -

   Apply ordination methods (PCA, NMDS, CCA, RDA)
   -

   Work with dissimilarity matrices and ecological distance measures
   -

   Analyse species?environment relationships
   -

   Use vegan effectively for real-world datasets

*Who should attend?*

Field ecologists, biodiversity researchers, and community ecologists who
already have some R experience and want to apply robust multivariate
methods to ecological data.

*Why join us?*

Deepen your statistical toolkit, sharpen your interpretation of community
data, and gain confidence in your analysis using vegan.

*Spaces are limited ? reserve your place now.*

 View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr08/>

Email oliverhooker at prstatitics.com with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] PR stats new social media - £2000 in course prizes available
messages.subject_clean: PR stats new social media - £2000 in course prizes available
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone,

We?re currently refreshing our social media presence and would really
appreciate your support. If you?re active on any of the platforms below,
please follow us and engage with any posts that catch your interest ? a
quick like or share can go a long way in helping us build momentum.

We?ll be posting regular course updates, plus running competitions and
giveaways.

To kick things off, we?re offering ?500 in course credit to one randomly
selected follower on each platform ? that?s ?2,000 in total! The more
platforms you follow and posts you like or share, the better your chances
of winning.

You can find us here:

*BlueSky* | @prstats.bsky.social
<https://bsky.app/profile/prstats.bsky.social>

*X (Twitter)* | @pr_stats <https://twitter.com/pr_stats>

*Mastodon* | @prstats <https://mastodon.social/@prstats>

*Instagram* | @pr._.stats <https://instagram.com/pr._.stats>

We?re also setting up our *Facebook* page. If you follow us now, you?ll be
automatically entered into the giveaway once we go live:

*Facebook* | PR stats
<https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61576148023294>

Thanks in advance for your support,

*Oliver*

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] National Science Foundation at 75 - free online conference
messages.subject_clean: National Science Foundation at 75 - free online conference
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a free 2-day conference, NSF at 75: Advancing Statistical Science for a Data-Driven World <https://instats.org/seminar/nsfa75-advancing-statistical-science-for>, livestreaming July 16?17 and jointly presented by the American Statistical Association and Instats. Celebrating 75 years of NSF support for transformative research, this conference brings together awardees, program officers, and disciplinary leaders who will showcase landmark advances in statistical methodology, highlight emerging priorities, and discuss why sustained federal investment is essential for both fundamental theory and real-world impact. Participants will gain an insider?s view of the value of NSF for science and society, and why proposed policy changes could threaten the vibrant research ecosystem NSF has long cultivated. Whether you are a researcher or policymaker, or simply someone who values the public good that flows from robust, well-funded science, this event offers insight into cutting-edge research funded by the NSF, as well as timely evidence, perspective, and dialogue on safeguarding the future of statistical science. 

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/nsfa75-advancing-statistical-science-for> and please share this with colleagues and students who care about the next 75 years of data-driven discovery!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Master Bayesian Species Distribution Modelling – Tailored for Ecologists
messages.subject_clean: Master Bayesian Species Distribution Modelling – Tailored for Ecologists
messages.body_plain:
*Master Bayesian Species Distribution Modelling ? Tailored for Ecologists*
ONLINE COURSE ? Species Distribution Modelling With Bayesian Statistics
Using R (SDMB07) This course will be delivered live
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-using-r-sdmb07/>

Ready to take your species distribution modelling to the next level?

Join our advanced online course:

*Species Distribution Modelling with Bayesian Statistics Using R*

*Course dates:* 30th Sept to 2nd Oct 2025

*Location:* Live and Online

*Instructor: *Dr Marcia Barbosa

This course introduces ecologists and environmental scientists to Bayesian
approaches for species distribution modelling (SDM) using R. You?ll learn
how to build, fit, and interpret SDMs using modern Bayesian tools and
ecological datasets.

Led by expert instructors, the course covers:

   -

   Bayesian model structure and thinking
   -

   Implementing SDMs in a Bayesian framework
   -

   Model fitting using R
   -

   Interpreting outputs and mapping predictions

*Who should attend?*

Ecologists, spatial analysts, and researchers with some experience in R and
basic SDMs who want to build models using a Bayesian approach.

*Why join us?*

Gain cutting-edge tools for ecological modelling, strengthen your
statistical understanding, and produce models that reflect the uncertainty
and complexity of real-world systems.

*Places are limited ? book early to secure your space.*

View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-using-r-sdmb07/>

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Evolutionary Macroecology in R: Exploring Biodiversity Patterns at Large Scales, online, June 2025
messages.subject_clean: Evolutionary Macroecology in R: Exploring Biodiversity Patterns at Large Scales, online, June 2025
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

There are a few slots available for the course: "Macroecology in R: 
Exploring Biodiversity Patterns at Large Scales."

It combines historical components of geographic patterns, phylogenies, 
and ecology to provide a comprehensive overview of evolutionary 
macroecology.

Places are limited to 16 participants.

Dates and schedule: Online live sessions from June 23rd to 27th, 2025, 
from 10:00-14:30 (Madrid time zone).

Course webpage: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/ecology/macroecology-in-r-exploring-biodiversity-patterns-at-large-scales/

COURSE OVERVIEW

This course provides a primer on Macroecology, focusing on geographic 
patterns of biodiversity and how those patterns are affected by species 
evolution and adaptation over time.

The course has a strong practical component in R. Students will learn to 
manipulate large geographic databases in R, grid-map biodiversity in 
various forms, conduct analyses of biogeographic regionalization 
incorporating evolutionary history, and estimate phylogenetic turnover 
and historical dispersal in geographic space. The practical sessions 
also cover common metrics in community phylogenetics and 
diversification, focusing on large geographic scales.

At the end of this course, participants will be able to:

  	* Generate geographic maps of biodiversity (species diversity, trait 
diversity, etc.).
  	* Use phylogenies to incorporate evolution into geographic patterns of 
diversity.
  	* Construct biogeographic regions while accounting for the 
evolutionary relationship of species.
  	* Map historical patterns of diversification, historical dispersal, 
and phylogenetic turnover.
  	* Calculate and map in-situ diversification, the age of assemblages, 
and new tip-based metrics of trait evolution.
  	* Associate geographic patterns of biodiversity with ecological 
predictors.
  	* Integrate tools of macroevolutionary dynamics, such as estimating 
ancestral areas and ancestral traits, with community phylogenetic 
metrics.

Several datasets will be provided to illustrate the application of these 
methods. Participants are encouraged to bring their own datasets to 
analyze and discuss with the instructor.

If you have any doubts or questions, do not hesitate to write to 
courses at transmittingscience.com

Best wishes

Sole

-- 
Soledad De Esteban-Trivigno, PhD
Director
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com/courses

Bluesky @soledeesteban.bsky.social
X @SoleDeEsteban
Orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2049-0890
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] ONLINE COURSE – Tidyverse for Ecologists (TIDY01)
messages.subject_clean: ONLINE COURSE – Tidyverse for Ecologists (TIDY01)
messages.body_plain:
ONLINE COURSE ? Tidyverse for Ecologists (TIDY01)

https://www.prstats.org/course/tidyverse-for-ecologists-tidy01/

We only have 3 places left on next week's course, Tidyverse for Ecologists!

16th - 20th June

Please feel free to share!

COURSE OVERVIEW - This course comprehensively introduces the Tidyverse and
focuses on its use in data science projects. It is designed to give
participants a strong foundation in R programming, core Tidyverse packages,
and the Tidymodels framework. The course emphasises hands-on projects to
apply learned concepts to real-world data analysis and modelling tasks
applied to biology. By the end of the course, participants should:
? Understand the fundamentals of R programming for data analysis.
? Be proficient in using core Tidyverse packages to clean, transform, and
visualise data.
? Gain an introduction to basic machine learning concepts through the
Tidymodels framework.
? Learn to preprocess, build, evaluate, and interpret models using
Tidymodels.
? Apply Tidyverse and Tidymodels tools to solve real-world problems
through hands-on
projects.

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

Day 1: A Short Course in R Basics (9:30 - 17:30)
This day provides participants with the foundational R skills required for
working with Tidyverse and
Tidymodels. It is designed for beginners or those needing a refresher in R
programming.
? Section 1 (R Essentials): This section focuses on R syntax, variables,
data types, conditionals (`if`,
`else`, `elif`), loops (`for`, `while`), and writing reusable code using
functions.
? Section 2 (Data Structures and File Handling in R): This section
emphasises understanding data
structures (e.g., vectors, data frames, lists) and handling files by
reading/writing data (e.g., CSVs)
for manipulation and analysis.

Day 2: Fundamentals of Tidyverse I (9:30 - 17:30)
This day introduces participants to the foundational concepts of Tidyverse
packages and their
applications to data science projects.
? Section 3 (Data Manipulation I): This section covers the basics of data
manipulation using `dplyr`
functions such as `filter()`, `select()`, `mutate()`, `arrange()`, and
`summarise ()`. Participants will
learn how to clean, transform, and prepare datasets for analysis.
? Section 4 (Data Visualisation I): This section introduces the principles
of data visualisation using
`ggplot2`. Participants will learn how to create basic plots such as
scatterplots, bar charts, and
line graphs while exploring the grammar of graphics.

Day 3: Fundamentals of Tidyverse II (9:30 - 17:30)
This day builds on the foundations established in Day 2 and dives deeper
into advanced data
manipulation and visualisation techniques.
? Section 5 (Data Manipulation II): This section extends the use of `dplyr`
by introducing more
complex operations such as joins, grouping with `group_by()`, and working
with pipelines using
`%>%`. Finally, additional packages will be presented to enhance data
manipulation
programming.
? Section 6 (Data Visualisation II): Participants will explore advanced
visualisation techniques
using extensions of `ggplot2`, such as creating animated plots with the
`gganimate` package and
interactive visualisations with additional tools.

Day 4: Applying Tidyverse Fundamentals to Data Modelling (9:30 - 17:30)
This day introduces participants to machine learning concepts using core
libraries for statistical modelling
and deep learning.
? Section 7 (Introduction to regression): This section focuses on
regression modelling using
Tidymodels. Participants will learn to implement linear regression models,
evaluate model
performance, and interpret results.
? Section 8 (Introduction to Classification): This section introduces
techniques such as support
vector machines and neural networks using Tidymodels. Participants will
also explore methods
for assessing the performance of classification models.

Day 5: Data Science Workflow with Tidyverse (9:30 - 17:30)
On the final day, participants will apply all their newly acquired skills
to solve real-world problems
inspired by ecological datasets.
? Section 9 (The data science workflow): The workflow will be illustrated
based on the core
packages introduced. The book "R for Data Science" will serve as
a base literature for this day
? Section 10 (Hands-on project): Participants will work through a complete
data science workflow,
including data cleaning, transformation, visualisation, modelling, and
communication of results.

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject_clean: Online Course – Design and Implement Species Monitoring in R (14–16 Oct )
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
registrations are now open for the Physalia online course:  Design, Implement & Promote Species Monitoring in R.
Dates: 14?16 October 2025

This course offers a comprehensive and practical introduction to the design and analysis of long-term species monitoring protocols using R. You?ll gain hands-on experience with robust and reproducible methods, including Census, Capture-Mark-Recapture (CMR), distance sampling, site occupancy, and N-mixture models.
 
 This course is ideal for students, researchers, NGO staff, and practitioners working in ecology, conservation, or environmental science. Prior basic experience with R is helpful but not strictly required ? all scripts will be provided and explained step by step.
 
 
For more information, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/species-monitoring-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/species-monitoring-in-r/ )

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course – Latent Class, Markov & Hidden Markov Models in R (2–5 Sept)
messages.subject_clean: Online Course – Latent Class, Markov & Hidden Markov Models in R (2–5 Sept)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
We?re pleased to announce our upcoming online course: Latent Class, Markov and Hidden Markov Models with Applications in R
 
 
Dates: 2?5 September
Time: 2?5 PM Berlin time daily
 
Instructor: Prof. Fulvia Pennoni (University of Milano-Bicocca, Italy) 
 Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/latent-class-markov-and-hidden-markov-models-with-applications-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/latent-class-markov-and-hidden-markov-models-with-applications-in-r/ ) 


This course will introduce the theory and practical applications of latent class, Markov, and hidden Markov models, particularly for the analysis of longitudinal data. It includes interactive hands-on sessions using R (MultiLCIRT and LMest libraries), and is suitable for researchers, students, and practitioners?even without a background in probability.
 
 Topics covered include:

Discrete latent variable models

Latent class models with covariates

Markov and hidden Markov models (with missing data handling)

Model selection and uncertainty estimation

Real-world case studies and exercises
Best regards,
 
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Causal Inference with Jeff Wooldridge - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Causal Inference with Jeff Wooldridge - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 2-day seminar, Causal Inference with Cross-Sectional Data <https://instats.org/seminar/causal-inference-with-cross-sectional-da>, led by professor Jeffrey Wooldridge, one of the world?s most renowned econometrics educators. Across two intensive days, you?ll build a solid, hands-on foundation for estimating causal effects when randomized trials aren?t feasible?mastering potential-outcomes notation, understanding the role of unconfoundedness and overlap, leveraging propensity scores, combining regression adjustment with weighting for doubly robust estimation, and extending these ideas to instrumental-variables, control-function, and regression-discontinuity designs.

This seminar runs prior to the next seminar in professor Wooldridge's series: Difference-in-Differences (DiD) Methods with Panel Data <https://instats.org/seminar/difference-in-differences-did-with-panel>. Conveniently, these seminars are being offered as a 4-part structured course on panel data modeling, From Static Models to DiD Estimation and Causal Inference <https://instats.org/structuredcourse/panel-data-modeling-from-static-models-4924> with professors Wooldridge and Marchese. The 4-part course includes two seminars first covering panel data methods to prepare you for professor Wooldridge's later seminars, with a 10% discount when purchasing them together.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/structuredcourse/panel-data-modeling-from-static-models-4924> to secure your spot, and feel free to circulate this announcement to colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

This beginner-friendly course is designed for researchers and practitioners looking to build a solid foundation in statistics using R. 
 
It includes a mix of lectures and hands-on exercises covering:

Data import, cleaning, and visualisation with tidyverse

Descriptive and inferential statistics

Linear models (regression, ANOVA), GLMs, and mixed models

Practical examples from biology and ecology using R
 
For the full list of our courses and workshops, please visit: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/fmc/ )
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] R for Beginners - Visualizing, Summarizing, and Exploring Data
messages.subject_clean: R for Beginners - Visualizing, Summarizing, and Exploring Data
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, R for Beginners (part 2) ? Visualizing, Summarizing, and Exploring Data <https://instats.org/seminar/r-for-beginners-part-2---visualizing-sum>, livestreaming June 19 (Europe) and led by the team at SMCS from the UC Louvain. Designed for researchers looking to strengthen their data-analytic toolkit, this hands-on workshop shows you how to harness R?s open-source power to conduct exploratory data analysis, compute key descriptive statistics such as means, medians, ranges, correlations, variances, skewness, and kurtosis, and create compelling graphics?from histograms, boxplots, and scatter plots to QQ plots, violin plots, mosaic plots, and Pareto charts. You?ll master the principles of effective visualization, learn to spot patterns and anomalies in complex datasets, and emerge ready to transform raw data into clear, actionable insights that guide informed research decisions.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/r-for-beginners-part-2---visualizing-sum> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Species Distribution Modelling (SDMs) and Ecological Niche Modelling (ENMs) using R (SDMR06)
messages.subject_clean: Species Distribution Modelling (SDMs) and Ecological Niche Modelling (ENMs) using R (SDMR06)
messages.body_plain:
Species Distribution Modelling (SDMs) and Ecological Niche Modelling (ENMs)
using R (SDMR06)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/species-distribution-modelling-using-r-sdmr06/>
Master Ecological Niche Modelling with R

*Dates:* 22?26 September 2025
*Format:* Live Online (Portugal Time Zone, GMT+1)
*Cost:* ?480
*Instructor: *Dr. Neftali Silero, Dr Jo?o Campos and Dr. Salvador
Arenas-Castro
------------------------------

*Course Overview:*

Unlock the power of Species Distribution Models (SDMs) and Ecological Niche
Models (ENMs) in this intensive 5-day live online course. Designed for
researchers and advanced students in spatial ecology, biogeography, and
conservation, this course offers a comprehensive introduction to building
and interpreting SDM's/ENMs using R.
------------------------------

*What You?ll Learn:*

   -

   Fundamental theory and methodologies behind SDM's and ENMs
   -

   Hands-on experience with R packages such as Dismo, Biomod2, and Ecospat
   -

   Application of modelling algorithms like Maxent, Bioclim, Domain, and
   logistic regression
   -

   Techniques for comparing and validating different models
   -

   Practical sessions using provided datasets and your own data

------------------------------

*Who Should Attend:*

   -

   Academics and postgraduate students working with spatial data
   -

   Researchers in biogeography, spatial ecology, or related fields with
   experience in ecological niche models

------------------------------

*Requirements:*

   -

   Basic understanding of statistical and ecological concepts
   -

   A laptop with R or RStudio installed (free and open-source software)

------------------------------

*Why Choose This Course:*

   -

   Live, interactive sessions with expert instruction
   -

   Practical, hands-on experience with real-world datasets
   -

   Opportunity to apply methods to your own research questions
   -

   Access to course recordings for flexible learning
   -

   Earn 3 ECTS credits upon completion

*Limited Availability:* Only 20 spots are available. Secure your place
today and advance your skills in ecological modelling.

*Register Now:*
https://www.prstats.org/course/species-distribution-modelling-using-r-sdmr06/

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] AI Tools for Researchers - free seminar
messages.subject_clean: AI Tools for Researchers - free seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a free seminar, AI Tools for Researchers <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-tools-for-researchers-free-seminar>, livestreaming on June 24th and led by professor Peter Gruber?an early adopter of large language models with dual PhDs in physics and economics. In this seminar professor Gruber will distill the most valuable AI tools for every stage of academic work. From sparking ideas and accelerating literature reviews to analyzing data, generating reproducible code, polishing manuscripts, and refreshing course materials, you?ll discover which tools are genuinely worth your time (and why) as well as how to weave them into your daily workflow, and what the future of AI holds for researchers and graduate students alike. You?ll even get a behind-the-scenes look at his personal AI setup, plus practical guidance on privacy, classroom use, and emerging applications that can simplify life beyond the office.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/ai-tools-for-researchers-free-seminar> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Using AI for Data Analysis in R  - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using AI for Data Analysis in R  - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

We still have a few places available for our 1-day seminar, Using AI for Data Analysis in R <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r->, running July 1st and led by professor Daisung Jang from Melbourne Business School. Designed for PhD students, academics, and research professionals, this workshop shows how generative AI tools such as ChatGPT and OpenRouter can be paired with R to accelerate data cleaning, analysis, visualization, and reporting. Through interactive demonstrations, Professor Jang will reveal how to write error-free code, automate repetitive tasks with custom functions, compare and select optimal packages, and annotate workflows for transparent, reproducible research?all while boosting productivity and opening new avenues for empirical inquiry. By the end of the day, participants will have hands-on experience integrating AI into their R projects, enabling faster, more insightful, and higher-quality results across disciplines.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/using-ai-for-data-analysis-in-r-> to secure your spot, and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!

Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
messages.subject_clean: Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
messages.body_plain:
*Advance Your Data Science Skills with Applied Spatial and Spatial Temporal
Modelling using R-INLA*

Are you looking to take your spatial data analysis skills to the next level?

Join our Spatial and Spatial-Temporal Modelling using R-INLA
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-and-spatial-temporal-modelling-using-r-inla-sstm02/>course,
designed for researchers and data professionals working with spatial and
spatiotemporal data in ecology, epidemiology, environmental science, and
beyond.

*Course dates:* 22-26 September 2025

Led by *Prof. Virgilio G?mez Rubio*, a leading expert in Bayesian spatial
modelling and author of *Bayesian Inference with INLA
<https://www.routledge.com/Bayesian-inference-with-INLA/Gomez-Rubio/p/book/9781138039872>*,
this 5-day course will give you hands-on experience with:

   -

   Building spatial and spatiotemporal models using the *INLA package in R*
   -

   Understanding model structures for real-world applications
   -

   Working with spatial datasets using real examples and guided exercises
   -

   Applying best practices for reproducible, interpretable analysis

*Why attend?*

Small group learning, direct access to an expert tutor, and practical
sessions designed to build confidence in implementing these advanced
methods.

Whether you?re a PhD student, academic researcher, or industry analyst,
this course will provide you with the skills and understanding to apply
spatial modelling in your work immediately.

*Places are limited ? book now to secure your spot.*

Full course details and registration
<https://www.prstats.org/course/spatial-and-spatial-temporal-modelling-using-r-inla-sstm02/>

If you have any questions, contact Oliver Hooker at:
*oliverhooker at prstatistics.com
<oliverhooker at prstatistics.com>*


-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] 16 of many Model Species Distributions with Bayesian Statistics in R | Online Course | 30 Sep - 2 Oc t
messages.subject_clean: 16 of many Model Species Distributions with Bayesian Statistics in R | Online Course | 30 Sep - 2 Oc t
messages.body_plain:
*Master Bayesian Species Distribution Modelling ? Tailored for Ecologists*
ONLINE COURSE ? Species Distribution Modelling With Bayesian Statistics
Using R (SDMB07) This course will be delivered live
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-using-r-sdmb07/>

Ready to take your species distribution modelling to the next level?

Join our advanced online course:

*Species Distribution Modelling with Bayesian Statistics Using R*

*Course dates:* 30th Sept to 2nd Oct 2025

*Location:* Live and Online

*Instructor: *Dr Marcia Barbosa

This course introduces ecologists and environmental scientists to Bayesian
approaches for species distribution modelling (SDM) using R. You?ll learn
how to build, fit, and interpret SDMs using modern Bayesian tools and
ecological datasets.

Led by expert instructors, the course covers:

   -

   Bayesian model structure and thinking
   -

   Implementing SDMs in a Bayesian framework
   -

   Model fitting using R
   -

   Interpreting outputs and mapping predictions

*Who should attend?*

Ecologists, spatial analysts, and researchers with some experience in R and
basic SDMs who want to build models using a Bayesian approach.

*Why join us?*

Gain cutting-edge tools for ecological modelling, strengthen your
statistical understanding, and produce models that reflect the uncertainty
and complexity of real-world systems.

*Places are limited ? book early to secure your space.*

View full course details and register here
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-species-distribution-modelling-with-bayesian-statistics-using-r-sdmb07/>

Please email oliverhooker at prstatistics.com with any questions

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online Course – Functional Trait Space Analyses in R (8–10 Sept)
messages.subject_clean: Online Course – Functional Trait Space Analyses in R (8–10 Sept)
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
The 2nd edition of the Physalia online course: Functional Trait Space Analyses in R
 
Dates: 8?10 September 2025
 
Course webpage: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/functional-trait-space-analyses-in-r/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/functional-trait-space-analyses-in-r/ )
 
 
This course offers a hands-on introduction to functional trait space analyses, combining theory with practical sessions in R. Participants will learn how to:

Build and interpret functional trait spaces

Apply PCA, kernel density methods, and key R packages (e.g. smatr, TPD, funspace)

Link trait spaces to ecological dimensions and visualize results
It?s ideal for PhD students, postdocs, and researchers working with functional traits, functional diversity, or trait-based ecology. Basic R knowledge is recommended.
 
 
Best regards,Carlo
 
 

--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] PR Stats Recorded Courses - 50% off and access extended to 90 days
messages.subject_clean: PR Stats Recorded Courses - 50% off and access extended to 90 days
messages.body_plain:
*Big Changes Coming to PR Stats Recorded Courses!*

We're revamping our recorded courses with brand-new features, improved
structure, updated content, and exciting new topics.

As we work on these upgrades, our recorded courses will be *unavailable
from July through August*.

To celebrate the changes, we're offering *50% off all recorded courses*
 purchased *before the end of this week* ? plus, you?ll get *extended
access until January 1st, 2026*.

Don't miss out on this opportunity to learn at your own pace and lock in
huge savings before the update!

General
*Advancing in R (ADVR01)*
<https://www.prstats.org/course/advancing-in-r-advrpr/>
Introduction to generalised linear models using R and Rstudio (IGLM06)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-generalised-linear-models-using-r-and-rstudio-iglm06/>
Introduction To Mixed Models Using R And Rstudio (IMMR07)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-mixed-models-using-r-and-rstudio-immr07/>
Model selection and model simplification (MSMS04)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-model-selection-and-model-simplification-msms04/>
Data visualization with ggplot2 using R and Rstudio (DVGG04)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/data-visualization-with-ggplot2-using-r-and-rstudio-dvgg04/>
Reproducible and collaborative data analysis with R (RACR03)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/reproducible-and-collaborative-data-analysis-with-r-racr03/>
Time Series Data Analysis (TSDA02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/online-course-time-series-data-analysis-tsda02/>
Time Series Analysis and Forecasting using R and Rstudio (TSAF01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/time-series-analysis-and-forecasting-using-r-and-rstudio-tsaf01/>
Introduction to Machine Learning using R and Rstudio (IMLR02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-machine-learning-using-r-and-rstudio-imlr02/>
Machine Learning with R (Intermediate - Advanced) (MLIA01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/machine-learning-with-r-intermediate-advanced-mlia01/>
Bayesian
Introduction / Fundamentals of Bayesian Data Analysis statistics using R
(FBDA01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-fundamentals-of-bayesian-data-analysis-statistics-using-r/>
Bayesian Approaches To Regression And Mixed Effects Models Using R and brms
(BARM01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bayesian-approaches-to-regression-and-mixed-effects-models-using-r-and-brms-barm01/>
Introduction To Stan For Bayesian Data Analysis (ISBD01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/introduction-to-stan-for-bayesian-data-analysis-isbd01/>
Bayesian Data Analysis (BADA02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bayesian-data-analysis-bada02/>
Spatial
Making Beautiful And Effective Maps In R (MAPR04)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/making-beautiful-and-effective-maps-in-r-mapr04/>
Adapting To The Recent Changes In R Spatial Packages (sf, terra, PROJ
library) (PROJ02)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/adapting-to-the-recent-changes-in-r-spatial-packages-sf-terra-proj-library-projpr/>
Using Google Earth Engine in Ecological Studies (GEEE01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/using-google-earth-engine-in-ecological-studies-geeepr/>
Remote sensing data analysis and coding in R for ecology (RSDA01)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/remote-sensing-data-analysis-and-coding-in-r-for-ecology-rsda01/>
Movement
Movement Ecology Using R(MOVEPR)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/movement-ecology-using-r-move07/>
Hidden Markov Models for movement, acceleration and other ecological data -
an introduction using moveHMM and momentumHMM in R (HMMMPR)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/hidden-markov-models-for-movement-acceleration-and-other-ecological-data-an-introduction-using-movehmm-and-momentuhmm-in-r-hmmmpr/>
Miscellaneous
Bioacoustics For Ecologists: Hardware, Survey design And Data analysis
(BIAC03)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/bioacoustics-for-ecologists-hardware-survey-design-and-data-analysis-biac03/>
Multivariate Analysis Of Ecological Communities Using R With The VEGAN
package (VGNR06)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/multivariate-analysis-of-ecological-communities-using-r-with-the-vegan-package-vgnr06/>
Stable Isotope Mixing Models Using SIBER, SIAR, MixSIAR (SIMMPR)
<https://www.prstats.org/course/stable-isotope-mixing-models-using-siber-siar-mixsiar-simmpr/>

-- 
Oliver Hooker PhD.
PR stats

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Using AI for Exploratory Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.subject_clean: Using AI for Exploratory Analysis - livestream seminar
messages.body_plain:
Hi everyone

Instats is excited to offer a 1-day seminar, Exploratory Analysis of Complex Datasets with ChatGPT <https://instats.org/seminar/exploratory-analysis-of-complex-datasets>, running Jul 14th and led by professor Nikolay Oskolkov from Molecular Biosciences, Lund University. Designed for scholars and professionals working with high-dimensional data, this intensive workshop will show you how to navigate the curse of dimensionality using both linear (PCA, MDS) and non-linear (tSNE, UMAP) dimensionality-reduction methods, deploy clustering techniques ranging from k-means and hierarchical clustering to GMM, DBSCAN, spectral, and graph-based approaches, and integrate neural networks and autoencoders to uncover hidden structure in genomics, AI, or time-series research. Combining rigorous theory with hands-on coding in R and Python, Nikolay will also demonstrate how ChatGPT can be woven into your analytic workflow to interpret complex outputs, generate insightful explanations, and streamline reporting?empowering you to tackle challenging datasets with confidence and creativity.

Sign up today <https://instats.org/seminar/exploratory-analysis-of-complex-datasets> and feel free to share this opportunity with colleagues and students who might benefit!


Best wishes

Michael Zyphur
Professor and Director
Institute for Statistical and Data Science
https://instats.org
	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online live course Geometric Morphometrics in R, July 28th – August 5th, 2025
messages.subject_clean: Online live course Geometric Morphometrics in R, July 28th – August 5th, 2025
messages.body_plain:
Dear colleagues,

I am excited to announce the 11th edition of the course "Geometric 
Morphometrics in R" (live online).

Geometric morphometrics is essential in ecology because it provides a 
precise and quantitative way to analyze shape variation, which is often 
linked to ecological adaptations and environmental pressures.

Dates: Online live sessions on July 28th, 29th, 30th, 31st, and August 
1st, 4th, 5th, 2025.

Daily schedule: from 12:00 to 17:00 (Madrid time zone).

Course webpage: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/geometric-morphometrics/geometric-morphometrics-r/

Instructor: Dr. Julien Claude, author of the book "Morphometrics with R 
[1]" (Institut des Sciences de l'?volution de Montpellier, France).

Course Overview

Concepts in geometric morphometrics will be taught using a series of 
original data sets and working in R for solving a series of tasks. The 
course will start with an introduction to R and will rapidly go into 
shape analysis with measurements, landmark data and outlines. The 
participants are welcome to bring their own data and problems so that we 
may find R solutions.

Best wishes

Sole

Transmitting Science's upcoming courses: 
https://www.transmittingscience.com/courses/

-- 
Soledad De Esteban-Trivigno, PhD
Director
Transmitting Science
www.transmittingscience.com/courses

Bluesky @soledeesteban.bsky.social
X @SoleDeEsteban
Orcid: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2049-0890

treatment by contacting Calle Gardenia, 2 Urb. Can Claramunt de Piera 
CP: 08784 (Barcelona) or sending an email to 
info at transmittingscience.com or 
http://transmittingscience.com/additional-terms. If you consider that 
the processing does not comply with current legislation, you can

Confidentiality. - The content of this communication, as well as that of
